summary: Jess Frazelle tests Transposit, a SQL interface for APIs, by rebuilding her 'Gitable' bot that syncs GitHub issues to Airtable. She walks through replacing complex 'glue code' and N+1 query problems with simple SQL joins across APIs. Despite attempting to break the platform with her 'weirdest' bot, the tool handles the complexity gracefully, including pagination and API rate limiting.
key_points: Transposit provides a SQL interface for interacting with multiple APIs simultaneously
The N+1 query problem in API glue code can be solved with SQL joins
Built-in API caching helps avoid rate limiting issues
Complex bot logic involving multiple APIs can be simplified significantly
SQL and Javascript operations can be combined for flexible workflows
money_quotes: Usually when I have a complex bot I end up writing a lot of 'glue code' to combine a few different APIs and get the information I want.
I fucking love Airtable. It's design just feels right and works the way my brain works.
Many APIs don't even have graphQL interfaces but also I am old school and I don't really want to learn something new.
Okay so now's the part where I am thinking… I'm going to break this thing. (Narrator: I didn't.)
It also caches the response which is a huge win because I am the queen of being rate limited.
key_insight: Complex API integration code with N+1 problems and pagination logic can be dramatically simplified using a SQL abstraction layer that handles the optimization automatically.
